title: Stop Prop Drilling Your React Modals (Use react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight utility that treats React components as callable asynchronous functions, eliminating prop drilling for modals. It introduces three core architectural patterns: Root and Stack for layout isolation, Upsert for active singletons, and Mutation Flow for backend loading states.
writeup: This video from Better Stack covers react-call, a lightweight utility that treats React components as callable asynchronous functions to remove prop drilling for modals. It matters because modal state and callbacks often thread through many layers of components, and modeling a modal as an awaitable call collapses that chain. The takeaways center on three patterns: Root and Stack for layout isolation, Upsert for active singletons, and Mutation Flow for backend loading states.
